title: Tool Definition
description:
Definition of a tool available to agents. Tools are external capabilities (APIs, services, utilities) that agents can invoke. The Runtime uses this schema to: 1) Inject tool definitions into LLM function calling, 2) Validate tool arguments before execution, 3) Provide helpful error messages for invalid calls.

TOOL vs CAPABILITY: A 'capability' in agent.schema.json grants permission to use a tool. A 'tool-definition' defines the tool's interface (what arguments it accepts). Both are needed: the studio defines tools, agents are granted capabilities to use them.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in function calling UI

description.description: What this tool does - included in LLM context to help it decide when to use the tool

cost_tier.description: Advisory cost tier for orchestration and UI. 'low' = cheap/fast, 'medium' = moderate cost, 'high' = expensive API calls, 'external' = triggers external services/commissions. Not hard budgeting - guides orchestrator decisions.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema defining the tool's arguments (function signature)

output_schema.description: Optional schema for the tool's return value (for documentation and runtime validation)

secrets_required.description: Environment variables the Runtime needs to execute this tool (e.g., 'OPENAI_API_KEY', 'DATABASE_URL'). Runtime validates these exist before enabling the tool.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum execution time in milliseconds before the tool call times out

examples.description: Example invocations to help agents understand usage patterns

terminates_turn.description: If true, calling this tool ends the agent's turn (yields control). The agent suspends and expects to resume later. This does NOT mean 'I'm done with my work' - it means 'yield control, expect to continue'. Runtime enforces: orchestrator agents MUST end every turn with a terminating tool call (e.g., delegate, communicate). Non-orchestrators may use prose output.

terminates_session.description: If true, calling this tool ends the entire session. This is the ONLY way to signal 'I'm done with my work forever'. Only meaningful for orchestrator agents. Use for explicit session completion or fatal errors. Most tools that terminates_turn do NOT terminate session - they yield control expecting to resume.

can_reject.description: If true, this tool may reject work even when execution succeeds (e.g., validation failures, permission denied). When can_reject=true and result contains action_outcome='rejected', it doesn't count as progress for iteration limits. Use for tools that validate or gate content.

summarization_policy.description: How to summarize this tool's result when context needs to be compressed. Used by the Secretary pattern to manage context growth.

summary_template.description: Template for concise/ultra_concise summaries. Variables in {braces} are replaced from tool result. Example: 'Delegated to {to_agent}' or 'Schema for {artifact_type} retrieved'.

tool_input_schema.description: JSON Schema for tool arguments. The Runtime validates agent tool calls against this schema before execution.

tool_output_schema.description: JSON Schema for tool return value. Optional - used for documentation and optional runtime validation.

requests_per_minute.description: Maximum calls per minute across all agents

requests_per_agent_per_minute.description: Maximum calls per minute per agent

summarization_policy.description:
Policy for summarizing tool results when compressing context:
- drop: Remove from summarized context (tool can be re-called if needed, e.g., schema lookups)
- ultra_concise: Single-line summary using summary_template (e.g., 'Delegated to Plotwright')
- concise: Brief multi-line summary preserving key facts
- preserve: Keep full result in context (default, for critical data)
